goal: Be conversational in Spanish for travel and basic work meetings within six months.
Incorporate roleplay scripts into weekly practice routine.
constraints: Weekly commitment: 45 minutes per day, six days a week.
Roleplay practice included in each 45-minute session.
decisions: Use a high-quality premade frequency deck to bootstrap core vocabulary.
Create own sentence-based cards focused on travel and work meetings as soon as possible.
Allocate 20–25 minutes per 45-minute session to SRS (Anki).
New cards per day: 4–7 (25–50 per week); adjust if reviews become heavy.
Gradually replace low-quality premade cards with self-made sentence cards or suspend unused premade cards.
Tag cards by topic (travel, meetings, grammar) for targeted review.
Include native audio when possible; use images for concrete nouns.
Use filtered decks to focus on urgent items; review via mobile app for quick bursts.
Paid live tutoring and/or subscription apps can accelerate speaking speed when combined with free tools.
Set a plan to evaluate cost-effectiveness (e.g., 4-week trial) and adjust based on progress.
Adopt daily shadowing as a pronunciation improvement technique.
Recommended paid session frequency: 2 paid sessions per week (25–30 minutes each) as a balanced approach; alternatives include 3 × 20 minutes or 3–5 paid sessions if budget allows.
Incorporate roleplay scripts practice into routine as practice prompts.
Adopt native content-with-transcripts ratio plan to progressively increase native input over six months (Month 1: 15–25% native-with-transcripts; Months 2–3: 40%; Months 4–6: 60%+).
entities: Easy Spanish Reader by William T. Tardy
First Spanish Reader: A Beginner’s Dual-Language Book by Ángel Flores
Spanish Short Stories for Beginners (Olly Richards)
Penguin Parallel Text: Spanish Short Stories (parallel-text collection)
La oruga muy hambrienta (The Very Hungry Caterpillar)
Roleplay scripts for travel and work meeting scenarios
open: Develop cost-effectiveness test plan: allocate budget for tutoring/subscriptions and evaluate impact over a 4-week period.
Arrange first conversation partner session(s) and schedule ongoing speaking practice.
Integrate a 10-minute daily shadowing routine into the study plan.
Create a beginner listening content plan: select 1–2 regular sources and implement a progression plan (Weeks 1–12).
Incorporate the Month 1/3/6 benchmarks into the study plan as milestone tests.
Schedule milestone checks around Week 4, Week 12, and Week 24 to assess progress.
Prepare test prompts for each milestone (vocabulary, listening, speaking, and writing tasks).
Plan to adjust the study plan based on milestone results.

Develop and implement a structured correction framework for practice with tutors/partners (set correction scope, balance immediate vs. delayed feedback, use reformulation/elicitation, create short scripts, and log corrections; add corrected sentences to Anki production cards).
Micro-task: Quick SRS + production (6–8 min) — Do your Anki review (including 2–4 new cards if you can) and immediately say aloud a sentence for 3 of the review items.

Micro-task: 1-minute shadow + record (6–9 min) — Pick a 30–60s clip or sentence from Dreaming Spanish/graded audio, do one shadow pass, record yourself repeating the same clip once, listen back quickly and note one thing to improve next time.
Micro-task: Targeted spoken drills (5–10 min) — Choose 3 practical prompts (self-intro, order food, ask for directions, meeting intro); speak each twice (unprepared, then corrected).
Gather user preferences on 1:1 vs group classes and rough weekly budget for tutoring to tailor platform mix and session frequency.
Create/refine roleplay scripts for practical situations: ordering food, asking for directions, checking into a hotel, and small talk.
